home.title: Free Student Housing Platform — Find & List Rooms
home.description: OpenHospi is the free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. Verified students only via SURFconext. No fees, no middlemen.

about.title: About OpenHospi — Our Mission & Story
about.description: Learn about OpenHospi's mission to make student housing in the Netherlands fair, free, and accessible for every student.

rooms.title: Student Rooms in the Netherlands
rooms.description: Browse available student rooms across Dutch cities. Free, verified, and fair for every student.

costs.question: Is OpenHospi really free?
costs.answer: Yes, OpenHospi is completely free for all students. There are no hidden fees, no premium plans, and no ads. We are funded by donations and sponsorships.

costs.question: How is OpenHospi funded?
costs.answer: OpenHospi is funded through individual donations, university sponsorships, and volunteer open-source contributions. All finances are transparent via Open Collective.

costs.question: Will OpenHospi ever become paid?
costs.answer: No. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win. No student will ever have to pay to use the platform or get more visibility.

safety.question: How are users verified on OpenHospi?
safety.answer: Every user logs in through SURFconext, which is managed by their own university or hogeschool. This means only real students of Dutch educational institutions can use the platform.

safety.question: Are my messages private?
safety.answer: Yes. All chat messages on OpenHospi are end-to-end encrypted. Not even OpenHospi can read your conversations.

safety.question: What happens when I report a user?
safety.answer: Reports are reviewed by our team. We take action quickly, including blocking users who violate our terms of service.

findARoom.question: How do I find a room on OpenHospi?
findARoom.answer: Log in with your SURFconext account, complete your profile, browse available rooms by city and price, and send a personal message to houses you like.

findARoom.question: What is hospiteren?
findARoom.answer: Hospiteren is the traditional Dutch way of finding housemates. Houses invite candidates for a visit so both sides can see if it's a good fit before moving in.

safety.title: Safety & Trust
safety.subtitle: Your safety is our top priority. Here's how we protect you.

features.title: SURFconext verification
features.description: Every user is verified by their own university or hogeschool through SURFconext. Your institution stands behind your profile. No fake profiles.

features.title: End-to-end encrypted chat
features.description: Messages are encrypted between sender and receiver. We cannot read your conversations.

features.title: No trackers
features.description: We don't use third-party trackers, analytics cookies, or sell your data.

features.title: GDPR compliant
features.description: We follow EU data protection regulations. You can export or delete your data anytime.

features.title: Open source
features.description: Our entire codebase is public. Anyone can audit how we handle your data.

features.title: Report & block
features.description: Report inappropriate behavior or block users with one click. We act on reports quickly. You can also reach us at abuse@openhospi.nl.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.description: OpenHospi exists to make student housing in the Netherlands fair, free, and accessible. We believe finding a room shouldn't cost money, and the process should be transparent. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win: no one can pay to get more visibility. Every student gets the same chances.

crisis.title: The problem
crisis.description: Students search for rooms through Facebook groups, Instagram, and paid platforms. On commercial sites, you pay to see listings or get priority. That's unfair — the system works for platforms, not for students.

builtBy.title: How it started
builtBy.description: Ruben saw fellow students struggling with Facebook groups, Instagram, and paid platforms. He didn't experience it himself, but saw many around him dealing with it. That's how OpenHospi was born.

openSource.title: Open source & community
openSource.description: OpenHospi is a community project. Anyone can contribute code, translations, or ideas. We're transparent about everything we do.

sections.title: 1. Controller
sections.content: OpenHospi is the controller for processing your personal data. OpenHospi is a free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. For privacy questions, contact us at privacy@openhospi.nl.

sections.title: 2. Privacy contact
sections.content: For all privacy and data protection inquiries, contact privacy@openhospi.nl. We aim to respond within 30 days, as required by GDPR Article 12.

sections.title: 3. Data we collect
sections.content: We collect the following categories of personal data:
items: Profile data — name, email, date of birth, gender, study information, photos
Housing data — room listings with addresses and coordinates
Communication data — end-to-end encrypted chat messages
Application data — personal messages and status
Session data — IP address, user agent for security
Push notification tokens — for delivery of notifications
Consent records — your cookie and privacy preferences

sections.title: 4. SURFconext data
sections.content: When you log in via SURFconext, we receive your name, email address, and educational institution domain. SURFconext is operated by SURF, the Dutch collaborative organisation for IT in education. We use this data to verify your student identity and create your account.

sections.title: 5. How we use your data
sections.content: We use your data solely to provide the OpenHospi platform: matching students with rooms, facilitating communication between seekers and hosts, and managing the housing application process. We do not sell your data, use it for advertising, profile you for marketing, or share it with third parties except as described in this policy.

sections.title: 6. Legal basis
sections.content: We process your data based on the following legal grounds. You can withdraw consent at any time via Settings > Privacy. See our Legal Basis Overview page for a complete breakdown per processing activity.
items: Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) — profile, housing, applications, chat; these are necessary to provide the service
Consent (Art. 6(1)(a)) — functional cookies, push notifications, analytics
Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) — session security (IP logging), platform safety (moderation reports)

sections.title: 7. Data sharing & processors
sections.content: We share your data only with the following processors, all located within the EU/EEA. All processors operate under Data Processing Agreements (DPAs). See our Data Processors page for details.
items: Supabase (Dublin, Ireland) — database and file storage
Vercel (Dublin, Ireland) — web hosting and edge functions
Upstash (Ireland) — rate limiting via Redis
SURFconext (Netherlands) — authentication

sections.title: 8. International transfers
sections.content: All your data is stored and processed within the European Economic Area (EEA). We do not transfer personal data outside the EEA. All our infrastructure providers are based in Ireland or the Netherlands.

sections.title: 9. Data retention
sections.content: We retain your data for the following periods:
items: Profile data, housing listings, and chat messages — retained as long as your account is active
Session IP addresses — anonymized after 30 days
Expired sessions — deleted after 90 days
Report message text — removed 90 days after resolution
Read notifications — deleted after 180 days
Consent record IP addresses — anonymized after 365 days
Account deletion — all data permanently removed via cascading deletes

sections.title: 10. Your rights
sections.content: Under GDPR, you have the following rights. You can exercise these rights via Settings > Privacy, or by contacting privacy@openhospi.nl.
items: Access your data (Art. 15) — via Settings > Privacy > Data export
Rectification (Art. 16) — edit your profile at any time
Erasure (Art. 17) — delete your account and all data
Restriction of processing (Art. 18) — pause processing while keeping data stored
Data portability (Art. 20) — export in JSON or CSV format
Objection (Art. 21) — object to processing based on legitimate interest

sections.title: 11. Right to lodge a complaint
sections.content: If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) at autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl, or with the supervisory authority in your EU member state of residence.

sections.title: 12. Cookies & tracking
sections.content: We use a minimal set of cookies. Essential cookies (session authentication) do not require consent. Functional cookies (language preference, sidebar state) require your consent under Dutch telecommunications law (Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a). We do not use tracking cookies or third-party advertising cookies. See our Cookie Policy for full details.

sections.title: 13. End-to-end encryption
sections.content: All chat messages between students and room hosts are end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) using AES-256-GCM with ECDH key exchange. This means OpenHospi cannot read the content of your private messages. Encryption keys are stored locally on your device and optionally backed up (encrypted) on our servers.

sections.title: 14. Children's data
sections.content: OpenHospi is exclusively accessible via SURFconext, which requires enrollment at an accredited Dutch educational institution. This means all users are students aged 16 or older, meeting the GDPR age requirement under Dutch law (UAVG Art. 5). We do not knowingly collect data from children under 16.

sections.title: 15. Automated decision-making
sections.content: OpenHospi does not use automated decision-making or profiling as defined in GDPR Article 22. Room matching is entirely manual — users browse listings and apply themselves. No algorithms determine who sees which rooms or who gets accepted.

sections.title: 16. Changes to this policy
sections.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Significant changes will be communicated via an in-app notification. The 'last updated' date at the top reflects the most recent revision. Continued use of OpenHospi after changes constitutes acceptance.

sections.title: What are cookies?
sections.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences and improve your experience. Under Dutch law (Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a), we need your consent for non-essential cookies.

sections.title: Essential cookies
sections.content: These cookies are strictly necessary for the platform to function. They do not require consent.
items: better-auth.session_token — authenticates your login session (session cookie, deleted when you close your browser)

sections.title: Functional cookies
sections.content: These cookies improve your experience but are not strictly necessary. They require your consent.
items: sidebar_state — remembers whether the sidebar is open or closed (persistent, localStorage)
NEXT_LOCALE — remembers your language preference (1 year)

sections.title: Analytics
sections.content: We use Vercel Analytics, which is cookieless and does not use personally identifiable information. While no consent is technically required, we list it in your consent preferences for full transparency. You can opt out at any time via Settings > Privacy.

sections.title: Managing your preferences
sections.content: You can change your cookie preferences at any time via Settings > Privacy in your account, or by clicking 'Manage preferences' on the cookie banner. For questions about our cookie policy, contact privacy@openhospi.nl.

sections.title: Eligibility
sections.content: OpenHospi is available to students of Dutch educational institutions that are connected to SURFconext.

sections.title: Account responsibilities
sections.content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account. Do not share your login credentials or attempt to impersonate others.

sections.title: Acceptable use
sections.content: You agree to use OpenHospi only for finding or offering student housing. Harassment, discrimination, fraud, and spam are prohibited.

sections.title: Content ownership
sections.content: You retain ownership of content you post (profile text, photos, messages). By posting, you grant OpenHospi a license to display this content on the platform.

sections.title: Liability
sections.content: OpenHospi is provided as-is. We are not responsible for the accuracy of listings, the behavior of other users, or any transactions made outside the platform.

sections.title: Governing law
sections.content: These terms are governed by Dutch law. Any disputes will be resolved in the courts of the Netherlands.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: If you have questions about these terms, contact us at support@openhospi.nl.

processingRegister.title: Processing Register (Art. 30 GDPR)
processingRegister.intro: In accordance with Article 30 of the GDPR, OpenHospi maintains this register of all personal data processing activities.

0.title: 1. User profiles
items: Purpose — Providing the student housing platform service
Data categories — name, email, date of birth, gender, study programme, institution, bio, lifestyle tags, preferred city, available from date, max rent
Legal basis — Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
Recipients — Other platform users (limited profile view)
Retention — Until account deletion
Security — RLS policies, encrypted transport, access controls

1.title: 2. Profile photos
items: Purpose — Profile identification
Data categories — up to 5 photos per user
Legal basis — Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
Recipients — Other platform users
Retention — Until deletion by user or account deletion
Security — Supabase Storage with access controls

2.title: 3. Room listings
items: Purpose — Housing marketplace functionality
Data categories — address, city, coordinates, rent price, room details, photos, availability
Legal basis — Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
Recipients — All authenticated users (or public for non-association rooms)
Retention — Until listing deletion or account deletion
Security — RLS policies, owner-only editing

3.title: 4. Applications
items: Purpose — Matching seekers with rooms
Data categories — personal message, application status
Legal basis — Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
Recipients — Room owner and house members
Retention — Until withdrawal or account deletion
Security — RLS policies restricting access to involved parties

4.title: 5. Chat messages
items: Purpose — Communication between seekers and house members
Data categories — encrypted message content, timestamps
Legal basis — Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
Recipients — Conversation participants only
Retention — Until account deletion
Security — End-to-end encryption (AES-256-GCM), only participants can decrypt

5.title: 6. Session data
items: Purpose — Authentication and security
Data categories — IP address, user agent, session token
Legal basis — Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) — platform security
Recipients — None (internal only)
Retention — IP address nullified after 30 days post-expiry; session deleted after 90 days
Security — Server-side only, not shared

6.title: 7. Moderation data
items: Purpose — Platform safety and abuse prevention
Data categories — reports, blocks, temporarily decrypted message text (for reported messages only)
Legal basis — Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) — user safety
Recipients — Admin team only
Retention — Decrypted message text deleted 90 days after resolution
Security — Admin-only access, audit logging

7.title: 8. Consent records
items: Purpose — Demonstrating valid consent (Art. 7)
Data categories — consent purpose, granted/revoked status, IP address, user agent, privacy policy version, timestamp
Legal basis — Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))
Recipients — None (internal audit)
Retention — IP address nullified after 365 days; records retained for compliance
Security — Immutable (no update/delete), RLS restricted to own records

8.title: 9. Push notification subscriptions
items: Purpose — Delivering push notifications
Data categories — endpoint URL, encryption keys
Legal basis — Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
Recipients — Browser push service
Retention — Until user unsubscribes or account deletion
Security — Web Push protocol with encryption

dpia.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (Art. 35 GDPR)
dpia.intro: This Data Protection Impact Assessment evaluates the risks associated with personal data processing on the OpenHospi platform.

0.title: 1. Description of processing
0.content: OpenHospi is a free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. Users authenticate via SURFconext (institutional SSO), create profiles, list rooms, browse listings, apply for rooms, and communicate through end-to-end encrypted chat. All users are verified students at Dutch educational institutions.

1.title: 2. Necessity and proportionality
1.content: Data collection is limited to what is strictly necessary for the platform to function.
items: Profile data enables matching (contract basis)
Location data enables geographic search
E2E encrypted chat ensures communication privacy
No data is sold or shared with third parties for marketing
No automated decision-making or profiling is used

2.title: 3. Risk: Profile data processing
items: Risk — Unauthorized access to personal profiles
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Row-Level Security (RLS) on all database tables. Authentication required via SURFconext. Profile visibility limited to authenticated users
Residual risk — Low

3.title: 4. Risk: Location data (room coordinates)
items: Risk — Precise location tracking via room coordinates
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Coordinates used only for map display. No location tracking of users. Coordinates are of the room, not the user. Only visible to authenticated users
Residual risk — Low

4.title: 5. Risk: Chat message content
items: Risk — Interception or unauthorized reading of messages
Likelihood — Very low
Impact — High
Mitigations — End-to-end encryption with AES-256-GCM. Per-message keys wrapped for each recipient. Private keys stored in IndexedDB (client-side). Server never has access to plaintext
Residual risk — Very low

5.title: 6. Risk: Photo storage
items: Risk — Unauthorized access to profile or room photos
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Supabase Storage with access policies. Photos only accessible to authenticated users. Deletion cascades on account removal
Residual risk — Low

6.title: 7. Risk: SURFconext data
items: Risk — Over-collection from SSO provider
Likelihood — Very low
Impact — Low
Mitigations — Only name, email, and institution domain are received from SURFconext. No additional attributes requested. SURFconext is managed by SURF (Netherlands)
Residual risk — Very low

7.title: 8. Risk: Moderation — temporarily decrypted messages
items: Risk — Exposure of private message content during moderation
Likelihood — Low (only on user report)
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Only the reporting user provides the decrypted text. Stored temporarily with 90-day auto-deletion. Admin-only access with full audit logging
Residual risk — Low

8.title: 9. Children's data (Art. 8)
8.content: All users authenticate via SURFconext, which requires enrollment at an accredited Dutch educational institution. This effectively ensures all users are 16+ (the Dutch UAVG threshold). No separate age verification is needed.

9.title: 10. Conclusion
9.content: The processing activities of OpenHospi present acceptable residual risks after mitigations. The platform implements privacy by design and by default through E2E encryption, RLS, data minimization, automated retention, and transparent processing. No high residual risks remain that would require consultation with the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens under Art. 36.

dataProcessors.title: Data Processors (Art. 28 GDPR)
dataProcessors.intro: OpenHospi uses the following third-party data processors. All processors are located within the EU/EEA. No personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area.

0.title: 1. Supabase (Database & Storage)
items: Role — Database hosting (PostgreSQL) and file storage (profile photos, room photos)
Location — Dublin, Ireland (eu-west-1)
Data processed — All personal data stored in the database, uploaded photos
DPA — Supabase Data Processing Agreement in place
Security — Encryption at rest and in transit, SOC 2 Type II certified

1.title: 2. Vercel (Web Hosting & Edge)
items: Role — Web application hosting, serverless functions, edge network
Location — Dublin, Ireland (eu-west-1)
Data processed — HTTP requests (IP address, user agent) in server logs
DPA — Vercel Data Processing Addendum in place
Security — Automatic HTTPS, DDoS protection, SOC 2 Type II certified

2.title: 3. Upstash (Redis — Rate Limiting)
items: Role — Redis database for rate limiting and abuse prevention
Location — Ireland (AWS eu-west-1)
Data processed — Anonymized rate limit counters (user ID hashes). No personal data stored
DPA — Upstash GDPR DPA in place
Security — Encryption at rest and in transit

3.title: 4. SURFconext (SSO/OIDC Provider)
items: Role — Identity provider for student authentication
Location — Netherlands (SURF infrastructure)
Data processed — Authentication tokens, name, email, institution domain (received during login)
DPA — SURF operates under Dutch law as an educational infrastructure provider
Security — SAML/OIDC protocols, institutional-grade security

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis Overview (Art. 6 GDPR)
legalBasis.intro: This page provides an overview of the legal basis for each data processing activity at OpenHospi, in accordance with Article 6 of the GDPR.

0.title: 1. Contract (Art. 6(1)(b))
0.content: The following processing activities are necessary for the performance of our contract with you (providing the student housing platform):
items: User profile creation and management
Profile photo uploads
Room listing creation and management
Application submission and processing
Chat messaging (E2E encrypted)
House membership management
Voting on applications

1.title: 2. Consent (Art. 6(1)(a))
1.content: The following processing activities are based on your explicit consent, which you can withdraw at any time via Settings > Privacy:
items: Functional cookies (sidebar state, language preference)
Push notification delivery
Analytics (Vercel Analytics — cookieless, no PII)

2.title: 3. Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f))
2.content: The following processing activities are based on our legitimate interest in platform security and user safety. We have balanced these interests against your rights and determined that the processing is proportionate and necessary.
items: Session data (IP address, user agent) — for security monitoring
Moderation data (reports, blocks) — for platform safety
Temporarily decrypted message text — for investigating reported messages

3.title: 4. Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c))
3.content: The following processing activities are necessary to comply with legal obligations:
items: Consent records (immutable audit trail) — to demonstrate valid consent per Art. 7 GDPR
Data request processing — to fulfill data subject rights (Art. 15-22)
Processing restriction records — to comply with Art. 18 right to restriction

4.title: 5. No automated decision-making
4.content: OpenHospi does not use automated decision-making or profiling as defined in Article 22 of the GDPR. Room matching is entirely manual — users browse listings and submit applications themselves. House members vote on applicants through a manual voting process. No algorithms determine who gets housing.

5.title: 6. Contact
5.content: If you have questions about the legal basis for processing your data, contact us at privacy@openhospi.nl. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) at autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl.
